title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence
summary: A two-zone service scales from noisy metrics, violates its recovery target during drain, and has enough replicas but insufficient failure-domain and resource headroom. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s capacity chaos recovery evidence increment in availability and autoscaling operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.
Compare vertical resource recommendation or mutation, in-place resize support, node autoscaling, workload disruption, scheduling, quotas, and provider dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence · Theory K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service has 6 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence · Theory K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test load, pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives. For Predict K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence · Theory K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, keep K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

For Predict K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence · Theory K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A successful kubectl drain and replica count prove the availability objective under real traffic.

options.text: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

A successful kubectl drain and replica count prove the availability objective under real traffic. That claim fails the predict k8s capacity chaos recovery evidence boundary for K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service has 6 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives. A defensible solution applies K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to availability and autoscaling operating policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence — Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-4
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s availability disruption autoscaling 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

# Evidence: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence-1yloon
  - competency: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml

    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query

    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
